name: Windsurf
pricingDetail: Free (25 credits/mo), Pro ($20/mo for 500 credits), Teams ($40/user/mo), and Max ($200/mo). Unlimited basic autocompletion.
description: An agentic AI-native IDE built on a VS Code foundation that treats AI as a first-class collaborator. It features the Cascade assistant, which maintains deep codebase awareness to perform multi-file edits, run terminal commands, and use a built-in browser autonomously.
keyFeatures: Cascade: Agentic AI for multi-step tasks and multi-file editing with full context.
Planning Mode: A live goal-tracking system that allows AI and developers to sync on long-term project objectives.
Windsurf Browser: A built-in, AI-integrated browser that lets the agent reason with live web content and UI previews.
bestFor: Developers seeking a high-context AI pair programmer capable of complex refactoring and autonomous task execution beyond simple autocomplete.
notIdealFor: Teams requiring strictly local, air-gapped AI processing or developers who prefer a minimal, non-IDE based coding environment.
recentUpdates: Full integration of Devin autonomous cloud agents, launch of Planning Mode for persistent goal tracking, and native support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
verdict: Windsurf is currently the most advanced 'agentic' editor on the market, offering deeper tool integration and better multi-file reasoning than traditional AI plugins.

name: Cursor
pricingDetail: Hobby (Free); Pro ($20/mo) includes $20 usage credits; Pro+ ($60/mo) includes $60 credits; Ultra ($200/mo) includes $200 credits; Teams ($40/user/mo). Annual plans available at 20% discount.
description: An AI-first code editor forked from VS Code that deeply integrates large language models to automate complex coding workflows and codebase-wide changes. It allows developers to build software using autonomous agents that can manage multi-file edits, debug in parallel, and learn project-specific rules.
keyFeatures: Composer 2 for autonomous multi-file editing and feature generation
Agent-first interface supporting parallel local and cloud agent execution
Smart codebase indexing for deep semantic search and context-aware suggestions
Interactive Canvases for visualizing dashboards, diffs, and real-time data within the editor
bestFor: Developers seeking maximum velocity and agent-led feature development within a familiar VS Code environment.
notIdealFor: Users who prefer a non-VS Code ecosystem or those who find credit-based usage billing too complex for their budget.
recentUpdates: Released Cursor 3 in April 2026 with a redesigned agent-centric workspace, integrated the proprietary Composer 2 model, and added 'Canvases' for interactive UI building.
verdict: Currently the gold standard for AI-integrated IDEs, successfully transitioning from a simple copilot to a powerful agent-first orchestration platform.
